verses.text: 'The king's heart is in the hand of the LORD,' turned wherever He wills; a man's way seems right to himself, but God weighs the heart; 'to do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.' God rules even kings. He values righteousness above ritual.

'The king's heart is in the hand of the LORD,' turned wherever He wills; a man's way seems right to himself, but God weighs the heart; 'to do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.' God rules even kings. He values righteousness above ritual.

A high look and proud heart are sin; the plans of the diligent lead to plenty, but haste to want; treasures by lying are vanity, and the robbery of the wicked destroys them. Pride and greed ruin. God blesses diligence and overturns ill-gotten gain.

The guilty man's way is froward, but the pure work right; better to dwell in a corner of the housetop than with a brawling woman; the wicked soul desires evil and shows no mercy. Peace is precious. The wicked crave evil even toward their neighbours.

When the scorner is punished, the simple grows wiser; the righteous wisely considers the wicked's house; 'whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry... but shall not be heard.' Mercy is required. To ignore the needy is to forfeit our own plea.

A secret gift pacifies anger; joy comes to the just when judgment is done, but terror to evildoers; he that wanders from understanding rests among the dead. Justice gladdens the godly. To stray from wisdom is to drift toward death.

He that loves pleasure, wine, and oil shall not be rich; the wicked become a ransom for the righteous; better to dwell in the wilderness than with a contentious woman. Indulgence impoverishes. Peace in solitude beats strife at home.

Treasure and oil are in the dwelling of the wise, but a fool spends it; he that follows righteousness and mercy finds life and honour; 'a wise man scaleth the city of the mighty.' Wisdom conserves and conquers. The pursuit of righteousness brings life.

He that keeps his mouth keeps his soul from troubles; the proud scorner deals in wrath; 'the desire of the slothful killeth him; for his hands refuse to labour.' Guard the tongue. Idle craving without work destroys the lazy man.

The covetous craves all day, but the righteous gives and spares not; 'the sacrifice of the wicked is abomination'; a false witness shall perish, but the obedient hearer speaks lastingly. Generosity marks the just. God rejects worship from an evil heart.

A wicked man hardens his face, but the upright orders his way; there is no wisdom against the LORD; 'the horse is prepared against the day of battle: but safety is of the LORD.' Preparation is wise, yet victory is God's. No human plan prevails against Him.
